name: plan_manifest_wave3_integration_verify
description: Run the stitched validation pass for registry-first control-plane manifests and fix only the integration fallout.

jobs.label: verify_and_fix_control_manifest_registry_slice
jobs.prompt:
## Goal
Run the integrated verification pass for the new registry-first control-plane manifest slice and fix only the breakage needed to get the slice coherent.

## Scope
The prior packets should already have implemented:
- storage owner support for status/lineage
- runtime control-plane manifest ownership
- data-plane manifest-id loading/persistence
- CLI/MCP surface wiring
- API raw-manifest boundary
- manifest list/search query lane

## Run these validations
```bash
PYTHONPATH='Code&DBs/Workflow' python3 -m pytest --noconftest -q \
  Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/unit/test_workflow_runtime_repository_control_manifests.py \
  Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/unit/test_control_plane_manifests.py \
  Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/unit/test_data_plane.py \
  Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/unit/test_cli_data_surface.py \
  Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/unit/test_mcp_docs_and_metadata.py \
  Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/unit/test_workflow_run_manifest_handlers.py \
  Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/unit/test_workflow_query_handlers.py
```

Then run the repo wrapper contract too:
```bash
./scripts/test.sh selftest
```

## If failures occur
- Fix only the files that are necessary to make this control-manifest slice coherent.
- Prefer minimal integration fixes over new abstractions.
- Do not widen the feature beyond control-plane manifests for data plans/approvals.

## Optional polish if tests are already green
If the surface docs/examples clearly lag the new manifest-id flow, update the minimal doc surface in scope.

Database: `postgresql://nate@127.0.0.1:5432/dag_workflow`
